run_policy.protocol: Wrap each query as a trigger-selection task: choose matching repository Skills from name and description, open each selected SKILL.md so loading is observable, report the selected names, and do not solve the underlying task.

cases.prompt: Audit our AGENTS.md and Skills as one guidance system. Explain why approval behavior differs across clients, and use loading order, precedence, and execution evidence before proposing revisions.
Review this existing SKILL.md as durable agent guidance. Go beyond wording issues and identify the root cause of its inconsistent behavior, with evidence and a validation plan.
The agent-facing reference docs contain the right policy, but agents do not follow it. Diagnose whether loading, authority, triggers, or distribution explains the gap.
Audit this Skill catalog for trigger conflicts, coexistence problems, and context cost. Separate what the files suggest from what runtime evidence confirms.
Audit approval boundaries and precedence across AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, SKILL.md, and relevant client policy surfaces. Compare the current system with structural alternatives.
Review this pull request diff for correctness, missing tests, security issues, and maintainability regressions.
Design a new Skill for rotating PDF pages, including its name, description, workflow, boundaries, and optional resources.
Create a new AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md document set for a repository that does not have agent instructions yet.
Review this Codex .rules file as a standalone command policy and tell me whether each allow, prompt, and forbid rule is correct.
Normalize this Markdown file to CommonMark, fixing heading spacing, lists, fences, and link syntax without changing its meaning.
